metadata.description: еЃЏе‡Ңж•° partial: The `functools.partial` function fixes some of the parameters of a function and returns a new callable object. Here is an example to illustrate this: ```python import functools # Define a sample function with three parameters. def sample_function(a, b, c): return a + b + c # Use functools.partial to fix the first parameter 'a' to 10. partial_func = functools.partial(sample_function, a=10) # Now partial_func can be called with only two arguments. result = partial_func(b=20, c=30) print(result) # Output: 60 ``` In this example, `functools.partial` is used to create a new function `partial_func` that always calls `sample_function` with the first argument fixed at 10.

data_pointers.title: еЃЏе‡Ңж•° partial

Here is an example to illustrate this: ```python import functools # Define a sample function with three parameters. def sample_function(a, b, c): return a + b + c # Use functools.partial to fix the first parameter 'a' to 10. partial_func = functools.partial(sample_function, a=10) # Now partial_func can be called with only two arguments. result = partial_func(b=20, c=30) print(result) # Output: 60 ``` In this example, `functools.partial` is used to create a new function `partial_func` that always calls `sample_function` with the first argument fixed at 10.
